nameMeanings.name: Franie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Perhaps anAmerican shortened form of Polish   Frankiewicz  .

variants.name: Franke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Fritz, Erwin, Otto, Armin, Wolfgang, Ernst, Guenter, Horst, Klaus, Siegfried.
German, Dutch: from the ancient Germanic personal name Franco (‘the Frank’); see Frank 1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Franie I. was born in 1440, in Devonport St Mary, Devonport, Devon, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son with Lady Elizabeth Jane Gorwyle Bluet. He died in Devon, England, United Kingdom.
